title: fleet expand subcommand (write-and-stop, --yes gate)
description: Add a sibling `fleet expand <goal>` subcommand in src/fleet/index.ts via a new registerFleetExpandSubcommand, leaving the existing `fleet <manifest>` registration byte-unchanged. It builds the DeepSeek client (credential fail-fast via createClient/validateApiKey BEFORE any write), calls decomposeGoal, assembles { rootDir, concurrency, children }, atomically writes it to <root>/.bober/fleet-expand.json (overwrite + notice), prints the manifest plus a review hint, and STOPS by default — chaining into the locked runFleet(outPath) only when --yes is passed. Wires options --count/--provider/--model/--root/--concurrency/--out/--yes.

successCriteria.description: The project builds successfully (tsc) with zero errors after the change.
TypeScript compilation produces zero type errors (strict mode).
ESLint reports zero errors on new/modified files (consistent-type-imports, no unused, ESM .js import extensions).

Running `fleet expand "<goal>"` without --yes (decomposeGoal + runFleet injected as fakes) writes a Zod-valid manifest to the resolved outPath, prints it plus a review hint, and the injected runFleet/coordinator is NEVER called (no spawn).
With --yes, the action calls runFleet(outPath) exactly once against the written file (verified via injected fake) and only after the manifest is written; without --yes runFleet is not called.

When DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is unset, the action surfaces a clear credential error (exit code 1 path) BEFORE writing any manifest file — asserted by confirming no file is written and the error originates from the client build.
registerFleetCommand still registers `fleet <manifest>` identically (line byte-unchanged) and `fleet --help` lists the `expand` subcommand; existing fleet/runFleet tests pass unchanged.

When the target manifest path already exists, expand overwrites it atomically and prints a notice naming the path; --out overrides the default <root>/.bober/fleet-expand.json; all existing tests pass.

nonGoals: No change to runFleet's signature/behavior, the `fleet <manifest>` command, FleetManifestSchema, or buildChildConfig
No interactive y/N prompt and no TTY check (--yes is the sole spawn gate)
No changes to decomposeGoal beyond importing it (built in Sprint 1)
No two-call decomposition; no hard child-count cap
stopConditions: All eight success criteria pass programmatic verification
Default invocation writes-and-stops with zero spawns; --yes invokes the injected runFleet exactly once; missing credential aborts before any write; `fleet <manifest>` behavior is provably unchanged
definitionOfDone: src/fleet/index.ts gains registerFleetExpandSubcommand(fleetCommand) (attached to the existing `fleet` parent, mirroring registerWorktreeCommand) wiring options --count/--provider/--model/--root/--concurrency/--out/--yes. The action: builds the decomposer LLMClient via createClient (fail-fast on missing DEEPSEEK_API_KEY before any IO), calls decomposeGoal, assembles { rootDir: opts.root ?? '.', concurrency: Number(opts.concurrency) ?? schema default, children }, atomically writes to opts.out ?? <root>/.bober/fleet-expand.json (overwrite + printed notice), prints the manifest + a 'Review then run: fleet <outPath>' hint, and on --yes calls runFleet(outPath) and prints the Fleet Summary; default exits 0 with no spawn. The `fleet <manifest>` registration line is unchanged. Collocated tests (injecting fake decomposeGoal + fake runFleet, and exercising the missing-credential path) cover write-and-stop, --yes chaining, credential fail-fast-before-write, overwrite notice, and `fleet <manifest>` regression. Build, typecheck, lint clean; all tests pass.
assumptions: decomposeGoal from src/fleet/decomposer.ts (Sprint 1) is imported and injectable for tests; the subcommand wires it to the real DeepSeek client in production.

createClient('openai-compat', 'https://api.deepseek.com', undefined, opts.model ?? 'deepseek-v4-pro', 'FleetDecomposer') throws via validateApiKey when DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is missing (src/providers/factory.ts) — this is the fail-fast, and it runs before any manifest write.
runFleet(outPath) is the locked Phase 1 entrypoint (src/fleet/index.ts:88) — reused via the on-disk manifest path, never modified.
The expand subcommand is attached to the existing `fleet` parent command following registerWorktreeCommand's parent+child pattern, so the `fleet <manifest>` line stays byte-identical.
Atomic write uses the existing temp+rename helper pattern (as in src/state/run-state.ts writeRunState); --out overrides the default path under <root>/.bober/.

Testing the action with DI: expose seams (e.g. an options object or module-level injectable refs) so tests can pass a fake decomposeGoal and a fake runFleet without real network/process spawning, following how fleet/index.test.ts injects FleetDeps into runFleet.
generatorNotes:
Edit src/fleet/index.ts (additive) and add collocated tests. Do NOT touch the `fleet ` registration line. 1. READ FIRST: src/fleet/index.ts (runFleet :88, registerFleetCommand :133, validateManifestCredentials, the Fleet Summary print, FleetDeps DI shape), src/cli/index.ts (registerWorktreeCommand parent+child pattern + where registerFleetCommand is called at :321), src/providers/factory.ts (createClient + validateApiKey), src/fleet/manifest.ts (FleetManifestSchema for the assembled object), src/fleet/decomposer.ts (decomposeGoal — Sprint 1), src/state/run-state.ts (atomic temp+rename write helper to mirror). 2. Add registerFleetExpandSubcommand(fleet: Command): attach `.command('expand ')` to the SAME `fleet` command object that registerFleetCommand builds (refactor registerFleetCommand to create the parent `fleet` command and call registerFleetExpandSubcommand(fleet) on it, OR export the parent so the expand child can be attached — keep the `fleet ` `.command('')` / `.action` lines byte-identical). Options: --count , --provider

, --model , --root

, --concurrency , --out , --yes. 3. action(goal, opts): (a) const model = opts.model ?? 'deepseek-v4-pro'; const client = createClient(opts.provider ?? 'openai-compat', 'https://api.deepseek.com', undefined, model, 'FleetDecomposer') // THROWS fail-fast if DEEPSEEK_API_KEY missing — BEFORE any write. (b) const decomposed = await decomposeGoal({ goal, client, model, maxRetries: 1 }) plus pass the --count hint into the goal/prompt. (c) const root = opts.root ?? '.'; const manifest = { rootDir: root, concurrency: opts.concurrency ? Number(opts.concurrency) : 3, children: decomposed.children }; (d) const outPath = opts.out ?? path.join(root, '.bober', 'fleet-expand.json'); ensure dir exists (mkdir recursive, async); atomically write (temp + rename) JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2); if the file already existed, print a notice that it was overwritten. (e) console.log the manifest + outPath + 'Review then run: agent-bober fleet ""'. (f) if (opts.yes) { const report = await runFleet(outPath); print Fleet Summary like the existing command } else { process.exitCode = 0; return; } // NO spawn without --yes. 4. Wrap action in try/catch mirroring the existing fleet command: on error logger.error + process.exitCode = 1. 5. For testability, factor the action body into an exported async function (e.g. runFleetExpand(goal, opts, deps?) where deps can inject decomposeGoal, runFleet, and the client builder) so tests avoid real network/spawn — mirror how runFleet takes FleetDeps. The commander .action just calls it. 6. ESM .js imports; import type for types; async fs only; no `any`; section comments. 7. Tests (collocated, e.g. src/fleet/expand.test.ts or extend index.test.ts): inject fake decomposeGoal (returns a known manifest) + fake runFleet (spy); use a temp dir for outPath. Cover: default writes manifest + does NOT call fake runFleet; --yes calls fake runFleet exactly once with outPath; missing-credential path (force the client builder to throw) writes NO file and exits 1; pre-existing file is overwritten with a printed notice; assert `fleet ` still registers/parses (commander program has both subcommands).
evaluatorNotes:
Independently verify each criterion.
sc-2-4: Run the default-path test; confirm a Zod-valid manifest is written to outPath and the injected runFleet/coordinator spy is NEVER called (no spawn). Inspect that the safety is structural — the only call into runFleet is guarded by opts.yes.
sc-2-5: Confirm --yes calls runFleet exactly once and only after the file write; confirm without --yes it is not called.
sc-2-6: Force DEEPSEEK_API_KEY unset (or make the client builder throw); confirm the command errors out BEFORE writing any manifest file (assert the file does not exist) and the error comes from the client/credential build.
sc-2-7: Confirm the `fleet <manifest>` `.command`/`.action` lines are byte-unchanged (diff the registration), that both `fleet <manifest>` and `fleet expand <goal>` are registered on the program, and the existing fleet/index tests pass unchanged.
sc-2-8: Confirm a pre-existing outPath is overwritten atomically with a printed notice and that --out redirects the write; run the full suite (zero failures).
Also check: no synchronous fs, .js import extensions, import type, no explicit any, no SDK import outside providers/. Confirm runFleet/FleetManifestSchema/buildChildConfig were NOT modified.
